new_classes.label_en: Wine vinegar, in containers holding > 2 l (CN 2209 00 19)
new_classes.label_de: Weinessig, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 Litern (KN 2209 00 19)
new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar obtained exclusively from wine (acetous fermentation of wine), presented in containers with a nominal volume exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2209 00 19.

coverage_explanation: The two primary discriminating criteria for CN 2209 00 19 are: (1) the product is wine vinegar (acetous fermentation from wine), and (2) it is in containers holding more than 2 litres. Both are captured via the WineVinegarQuality restriction and the LargeContainer (≥ 2 L) part restriction. The legal note references Additional Note 11 to Chapter 22 (which sets out the boundary between wine vinegar and other vinegar, and the container volume threshold for 22090011 vs. 22090019) and HS Explanatory Notes for heading 2209. These are correctly modelled. The coverage score is reduced slightly from 1.0 because the exact threshold for 'wine vinegar' quality (i.e. produced exclusively by acetous fermentation of wine) and Additional Note 11 specifics are referenced textually but not fully axiomatised in formal OWL beyond the named quality class. The disjointness with 22090011 (small container) is expressed via a complement restriction to reinforce mutual exclusivity of the sibling codes.
